predictions.text: Platts/S&P Global will assess the JKM LNG spot price at or above $25.00 per MMBtu on at least one publication day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if S&P Global Commodity Insights (Platts) publishes a JKM (Japan-Korea Marker) daily assessment >= 25.00 USD/MMBtu at least once on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Current conflict elevates Hormuz risk; LNG is flagged as potentially more vulnerable than oil if shipping is disrupted.
Energy markets are already repricing stagflation risk and supply disruption tail outcomes.
monitoring_signals: Any announced or observed disruptions to LNG transits near Hormuz
JKM forward curve steepening/backwardation and widening prompt spreads
Asian spot procurement tenders and emergency cargo re-routing
Reports of higher LNG shipping insurance premia
predictions.rationale: If Hormuz risk intensifies, marginal LNG cargoes can reprice sharply due to limited short-run flexibility. Threshold chosen to be meaningfully above typical non-crisis levels but plausible under sustained disruption.

predictions.text: Baltic Exchange will publish a VLCC Middle East Gulf–China (TD3C) time-charter equivalent (TCE) rate at or above $100,000/day on at least one day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the Baltic Exchange’s published TD3C (VLCC MEG–China) TCE is reported >= 100000 USD/day at least once on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Largest-ever oil supply disruption framing implies strong tanker demand and rerouting risk.
War-risk concerns and potential convoying/avoidance can reduce effective tanker supply.
monitoring_signals: Baltic TD3C TCE and Worldscale prints trend
War-risk insurance quotes and listed-area expansions
Diversions via Cape of Good Hope and longer voyage distances
Port congestion and floating storage indicators
predictions.rationale: Even without full closure, heightened risk can create a scarcity premium in effective tonnage and spike spot earnings; $100k/day is a high but achievable stress-level print.

predictions.text: The International Energy Agency (IEA) will announce a coordinated member release totaling at least 60 million barrels (oil and/or products) explicitly tied to the Middle East conflict.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the IEA issues an official statement/press release announcing a 'collective action' (or equivalent coordinated release) with a stated total volume >= 60 million barrels and explicitly cites the Middle East conflict/supply disruption as a driver, by 2026-05-31; otherwise NO.
evidence: G7 energy ministers are reported to be considering coordinated strategic reserve releases to stabilize prices.
monitoring_signals: IEA/G7 energy minister communiqués and meeting readouts
Sharp rise in crude/product benchmarks and physical differentials
Political statements on inflation relief and supply stabilization
Member-country announcements that reference IEA coordination
predictions.rationale: Coordination risk is elevated when price spikes threaten macro stability; 60mb is large enough to be distinguishable from small national actions and consistent with prior IEA collective actions.

predictions.text: ICE Endex Dutch TTF natural gas front-month futures will settle at or above €80.00/MWh on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the ICE Endex Dutch TTF Gas Futures front-month contract has an official daily settlement price >= 80.00 EUR/MWh at least once on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Analysts flag LNG vulnerability under Hormuz disruption, which can transmit to European gas balances via LNG competition.
Broader energy-market whipsaws and supply-shock narratives are active.
monitoring_signals: European LNG send-out and cargo arrival cancellations/delays
TTF prompt spreads widening and implied volatility
EU storage trajectory versus seasonal norms
Asian LNG price spikes pulling cargoes away from Europe
predictions.rationale: Europe is exposed through global LNG competition; a sustained risk premium could lift TTF materially. €80/MWh is a stress marker that indicates significant global LNG tightness.

predictions.text: The ICE BofA MOVE Index will close at or above 160 on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the ICE BofA MOVE Index (ticker MOVE) official close is >= 160.00 on any trading day on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Treasury yields are reacting sharply to war news and de-escalation signals, highlighting geopolitical dominance over macro data.
monitoring_signals: Large intraday rate swings on headlines (ceasefire/blockade/attacks)
Skew/vol in Treasury options markets
Funding/liquidity stress in UST market functioning indicators
Inflation swap volatility and breakeven volatility measures
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical headline risk can create episodic rate-vol spikes; 160 is a historically elevated level consistent with acute macro/geopolitical uncertainty but not an extreme tail like crisis peaks.

predictions.text: The S&P 500 Index (SPX) will experience a peak-to-trough drawdown of at least 15.0% measured from the 2026-03-03 closing level to any subsequent intraday low.
predictions.verification_criteria: Let C be the official SPX close on 2026-03-03. Resolves YES if SPX trades intraday at or below C*(1-0.15) at any time on or before 2026-06-30 (using official index levels); otherwise NO.
evidence: Market narratives cite stagflation/recession fears linked to the oil shock and supply disruption.
Equities are reacting to war-end signals and blockade threats with sharp repricing.
monitoring_signals: Oil and gas spikes alongside widening credit spreads
VIX term structure inversion and equity downside skew
Earnings pre-announcements citing energy/transport disruptions
Macro surprise indices turning sharply negative
predictions.rationale: A large energy shock combined with geopolitical uncertainty can plausibly push equities into a correction. The 15% threshold is meaningful and avoids trivial volatility while remaining plausible within a few months.

predictions.text: Lloyd’s Market Association (Joint War Committee) will issue a listed-areas update adding at least one new area in the Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz region or materially expanding the boundaries of an existing listed area there.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the LMA Joint War Committee publishes an official circular/update before 2026-04-15 that (a) newly lists any area within the Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz OR (b) expands boundary coordinates of an already-listed area in that region; otherwise NO.
evidence: Tanker traffic warnings and blockade threats imply elevated maritime risk that typically propagates into insurance and listed-area actions.
monitoring_signals: Reported attacks/seizures near shipping lanes
War-risk premium spikes reported by brokers
Naval advisories and changes to recommended transit practices
LMA/JWC circular frequency and wording changes
predictions.rationale: Listed-area changes are a common institutional response when risks rise fast. The near-term deadline captures the likelihood of insurance-market repricing if threat conditions worsen.

predictions.text: At least three European countries will jointly announce (single coordinated statement or simultaneous official releases) a new or expanded maritime security operation explicitly aimed at protecting commercial shipping in/near the Strait of Hormuz.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by 2026-04-30, official government/defense ministry statements show at least three European countries (e.g., France/UK/Italy/Spain/Netherlands/Germany) jointly announcing a new or expanded operation/mission with an explicit mandate to protect/escort commercial shipping in/near the Strait of Hormuz (operation name/command structure not required but must be explicit); otherwise NO.
evidence: France has deployed a major naval force to the Middle East, signaling heightened European engagement in sea-lane protection.
monitoring_signals: EU/UK/French MoD briefings and parliamentary statements
Merchant shipping advisories requesting naval coordination
Announcements of escort/convoy protocols
Increases in European naval deployments and basing arrangements
predictions.rationale: European naval deployments often crystallize into formalized missions when commercial risk and insurance costs surge. Three-country threshold avoids counting purely national deployments while remaining feasible under acute sea-lane risk.

predictions.text: The UN Security Council will adopt a resolution (not merely a statement) calling for de-escalation/ceasefire related to the U.S.–Iran conflict, passing with at least 10 votes in favor.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, on or before 2026-06-30, the UN Security Council adopts a resolution referencing the U.S.–Iran conflict (or hostilities involving Iran and the U.S./allies) that includes operative language calling for ceasefire/de-escalation, and the official voting record shows >=10 votes in favor; otherwise NO.
evidence: Escalation and sea-lane risk have increased international pressure for formal de-escalation mechanisms.
monitoring_signals: Draft resolutions circulated by UNSC members
Public positions by P5 members and veto threats
Emergency UNSC sessions and readouts
Ceasefire mediation efforts by third parties
predictions.rationale: A UNSC resolution is plausible under severe global spillovers but constrained by veto politics. A low-to-moderate probability reflects institutional friction despite strong incentives to reduce energy and maritime disruption.
